title: rename tables causes duplicated row in information_schema.columns
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

``` SQL
create table t1(a int);
create table t2(a int);
create table t3(a int);
create database test1;
rename table test.t1 to test1.t1, test.t2 to test1.t2, test.t3 to test1.t3;
select column_name from information_schema.columns where table_name = 't1';
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

``` 
+-------------+
| column_name |
+-------------+
| a           |
+-------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
```
+-------------+
| column_name |
+-------------+
| a           |
| a           |
+-------------+
```
### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
master
<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t4(b VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE t5(c FLOAT DEFAULT NULL);
CREATE TABLE t6(d DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE);
ALTER TABLE t4 RENAME TO test2.t4;
ALTER TABLE t5 RENAME TO test2.t5;
key_bug_features: rename table operation in information_schema.columns query
